problem.title: Implement a Stack Using an Array
problem.description: Implement a stack data structure using an array with push, pop, and peek operations.

example.input: push(1), push(2), top() → 2, pop() → 2, empty() → false
example.explanation: LIFO: Last In First Out. All operations O(1) with array.

solutions.title: Solution 1 – Array Implementation
solutions.description: Back the stack with a dynamically resizing array: when the array is full, allocate a new array of double the capacity and copy elements over. A top pointer tracks the index of the most recently pushed element, and all core operations work in O(1) amortized time. The key insight is that doubling the capacity on resize limits the total number of copy operations to O(n) across all pushes, keeping the amortized cost constant.
